item.title: Multi Label Classification Using Pattern Exploiting Training
item.description: Multi-label Classification using Pattern Exploiting Training Introduction Pattern Exploiting Training We start with a brief introduction to Pattern Exploiting Training(PET). PET is a semi-supervised training method, it reformulates input as cloze questions to help language models better understand the semantics. For a mathematics definition, let $M$ be the language model with vocabulary $V$ and mask token $__ \in V$. We define pattern, which is a function $P()$ that takes input $x$ and outputs a phase $P(x)$ that only contains 1 masks.

item.title: About
item.description: This is Zirui Li, a Master’s student from the TU Delft. I obtained my Bachelor’s degree in Tianjin University. I was a Machine Learning Engineering intern in Cambricon, where I was working on Neural Network Quantization. Currently, I am interested in Inclusive Automatic Speech Recognition. I am supervised by Odette Scharenborg on my graduate thesis, working on Inclusive ASR.
